summary: Follow-up frontend test of the WebRTC TURN + localVideoRef fixes in MessagesView.jsx. All STATIC checks pass. All UI-level regressions pass. End-to-end media flow (mic/cam) cannot be exercised in headless Chromium (no devices) — main agent must confirm on a real client machine.

static_verification.localVideoRef_useEffect: PASS — useEffect at lines 315-320 watches [call?.video, call?.active, call?.incoming] and assigns localStreamRef.current -> localVideoRef.current.srcObject (with a race-safe `srcObject !== localStreamRef.current` guard)
static_verification.debug_logs_wired: PASS — pc.onconnectionstatechange / oniceconnectionstatechange / onicegatheringstatechange / ontrack all console.log with '[call] …' prefix in BOTH startCall (lines 333-338) and acceptCall (lines 367-372)
static_verification.poller_dedupe: PASS — messages merged via Array.from(new Map(raw.map(x=>[x.id,x])).values()) at line 105
static_verification.grid_layout: PASS — <video> grid renders only when call.video === true (line 618). remoteVideoRef is NOT muted, localVideoRef IS muted (line 621) — correct for echo avoidance.

runtime_verification.login_and_navigation: PASS — testbugfix1 / password1 login with math captcha (12-9=3) → /client/dashboard, then sidebar Messages opens /messages-user-search
runtime_verification.case_insensitive_search_balkin: PASS — typing 'balkin' shows Balkin OWNER row in dropdown (regression check for Bug 3 still green)
runtime_verification.text_dm_right_aligned: PASS — 'regression hello owner' bubble renders inside .justify-end container
runtime_verification.duplicate_key_warnings: 0 warnings across the whole run — dedup fix holds
runtime_verification.call_audio_btn_click: Toast 'Can\'t access mic/cam: Requested device not found' appears then modal auto-closes via endCall(). Expected in headless (no mic device). Graceful error path confirmed.
runtime_verification.call_video_btn_click: Same behaviour — toast + modal auto-closes. Two <video> elements ONLY render when getUserMedia succeeds, which is never in headless; static file check confirms they are wired.
runtime_verification.iceGatheringState_debug_log_observed: NOT OBSERVED at runtime — because pcRef is created AFTER the awaited getUserMedia call, and getUserMedia rejects in headless. This is by design; on a real machine with mic/cam these logs will fire. Static wiring is correct.

action_items: END-TO-END call test can only be done with two real browser sessions on machines with mic/cam. Main agent should ask the user (or run on the VPS staging) to open the app in two browsers as two users and verify: (a) [call] iceGatheringState / iceConnectionState / connectionState / ontrack logs appear in DevTools, (b) both local & remote video tiles show live video, (c) remote audio is audible.

OpenRelay is a public shared TURN — under load it can rate-limit. If production video calls occasionally fail, replace with Twilio Network Traversal or Metered.ca paid credentials via ENV vars instead of hardcoding in MessagesView.jsx.

Minor: rtcConfig currently lives in the render body — it's re-created on every render. Move it to module scope (outside the component) to avoid needless object churn on each RTCPeerConnection instantiation.
critical_code_review_comments: TURN credentials 'openrelayproject/openrelayproject' are hardcoded in the JS bundle. That's public info for OpenRelay so acceptable, but consider moving to REACT_APP_TURN_URL / _USERNAME / _CREDENTIAL env vars so production can switch providers without a code change.
startCall/acceptCall silently swallow errors on sendSignal via .catch(()=>{}) — an authenticated session that has expired mid-call would cause silent failure. Consider surfacing 401 as a toast.

The [call] console.log spam is fine for debugging but should be gated behind a debug flag (e.g., if (window.__DEBUG_CALL) console.log(...)) before production release — otherwise every user session logs iceGatheringState transitions to the DevTools console.
rtcConfig object literal is inside the component — moves it to module scope for a small perf win and to make it easier to override for tests.

success_rate.frontend: Static checks: 100% PASS. UI/regressions: 100% PASS. E2E media-flow: INCONCLUSIVE (no hardware in headless — expected).

test_credentials: testbugfix1 / password1 — verified. Login route is /client/login, dashboard is /client/dashboard.
seed_data_creation: Sent one DM ('regression hello owner') from testbugfix1 to Balkin during the run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login route is /client/login, dashboard is /client/dashboard (NOT /login and /dashboard). Sidebar has a 'Messages' link that goes to MessagesView. Math captcha renders inside [data-testid=login-captcha] .select-none (question text) and [data-testid=login-captcha-answer] (input). Headless Chromium in this sandbox has NO mic/cam — getUserMedia always rejects NotFoundError, so any WebRTC end-to-end path (PC creation, ICE, ontrack, media playback) CANNOT be verified here. Verify statically in MessagesView.jsx or ask user to test on real hardware.
